description:
You are acting as the new “artifact–manager” for a small-scale binary repository that lives on the local
  workstation.  Your goal is to automate the periodic removal of incomplete uploads (files that end in
  “.tmp” or “.partial”) and to keep a clear audit trail of what was removed and when it happened.

  Initial facts you can rely on
    • The repository to curate already exists at /home/user/binary-repo  
    • Inside that directory there are a few normal packages plus two
      incomplete files whose names end in “.tmp” and “.partial”.  
    • No clean-up script or cron entry has been created yet.

  What you must deliver
    1. Directory layout  
         • Create /home/user/artifact-tools          (for scripts & logs)  
    2. Clean-up script                              (exact path must match)  
         • Path:  /home/user/artifact-tools/cleanup_repo.sh  
         • The first line MUST be a POSIX she-bang (#!/usr/bin/env bash or similar).  
         • Hard-code the variable  REPO_DIR=/home/user/binary-repo  
         • The script shall:  
             a. Record the current local timestamp into a variable START_TS  
             b. Count the number of files about to be deleted:
                     all files in $REPO_DIR whose names end in “.tmp” or “.partial”  
             c. Remove those files (rm –f)  
             d. Record another timestamp into FINISH_TS  
             e. Append exactly THREE lines to one log file
                (/home/user/artifact-tools/cleanup_repo.log) using the
                format shown below.  No extra whitespace, no extra lines.
                   START:  YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
                   DELETED: <integer>
                   FINISH: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
                (START: and FINISH: timestamps must reflect START_TS and
                 FINISH_TS respectively.)  
         • Make the script executable by the current user.  
    3. Cron job  
         • Install a user crontab entry so the clean-up script executes
           every day at 04:45 **local time**.  
         • The single crontab line must be exactly
               45 4 * * * /home/user/artifact-tools/cleanup_repo.sh >> /home/user/artifact-tools/cleanup_repo.log 2>&1
    4. First manual run  
         • Execute the script once immediately so that the first three-line
           block is present in /home/user/artifact-tools/cleanup_repo.log.  
    5. Verification hints  
         • After the run, there must be NO “.tmp” or “.partial” files left
           anywhere inside /home/user/binary-repo.  
         • The log file must exist and its last three lines must follow the
           exact format above and report
                DELETED: 2
           (because two incomplete files existed initially).  
         • Your personal user crontab (not /etc/crontab) must now contain
           the one exact line shown in step 3.  

  The automated tester will verify:

    a. /home/user/artifact-tools/cleanup_repo.sh exists, is executable and
       contains the word “REPO_DIR=/home/user/binary-repo”.
    b. /home/user/artifact-tools/cleanup_repo.log exists and its most
       recent three lines match the required pattern, with the second line
       reading exactly “DELETED: 2”.
    c. crontab -l contains the exact line given in step 3.
    d. find /home/user/binary-repo -type f \( -name '*.tmp' -o -name '*.partial' \)
       returns nothing.

  Perform all necessary terminal operations to satisfy the above.  Do
  NOT modify any other system locations.  When you are finished, show the
  last three lines of the log to confirm success.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem state created by the grader before the agent
  starts:

    1. Directory /home/user/binary-repo (permissions 755) contains:
          alpha-1.0.0.jar
          alpha-1.0.1.jar
          beta-2.2.3.tmp
          gamma-0.9.1.partial
          readme.txt

    2. /home/user/artifact-tools does NOT exist.

  What the grader will assert AFTER the agent finishes:

    • /home/user/artifact-tools/cleanup_repo.sh  
        – regular file, executable.  
        – contains line beginning with “REPO_DIR=/home/user/binary-repo”.  

    • /home/user/artifact-tools/cleanup_repo.log  
        – last three lines (tail -n 3) match regex:
            ^START: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$
            ^DELETED: 2$
            ^FINISH: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$

    • User crontab contains exactly the line:
        45 4 * * * /home/user/artifact-tools/cleanup_repo.sh >> /home/user/artifact-tools/cleanup_repo.log 2>&1

    • No file in /home/user/binary-repo has extension .tmp or .partial.
